figures.caption: Fig. 4. The results of experiment 1. The proposed QP-VF (left column) results in a higher average speed (vavg, dashed line). The baseline (right column) exhibits highly saturated angular control. The bottom plots experimentally validate Proposition 5: the angular error ϕ, which started from the unsafe set (|ϕ| > π/2), is driven into the safe set and remains there.

Fig. 5. The results of experiment 2. These plots demonstrate the selfregulating loop discussed in Section V. The proposed QP-VF (left column) allows for a higher average speed (vavg, dashed line) compared to the baseline (right column).
TABLE I VECTOR FIELD QUALITY COMPARISON. ALL VALUES ARE MEAN ± STD DEV.
Fig. 2. comparison of integral curves for the baseline (left), and proposed QP (right) methods across two environments.
